coverage_notes: Qualitative / manual triage: vague user language for blueprint editing, delegation vs main-agent tools, T3D-ish workflows, and recap. Not a strict pass/fail suite—use classify_harness_run_jsonl.py and run.jsonl for Blueprint Builder chain events, gate blocks, and tool_finish rates.

turns.request: I'm iterating on gameplay feel. Create a fresh actor blueprint somewhere under /Game/AIHarness/BlueprintBuilderEdges/ with a short memorable name, parented off a plain Actor, so we can mess with it without touching shipped assets.
When that actor runs, I want a clear sign it fired—either an on-screen message or a log line on startup. Wire it up the way this project's system prompt recommends for graph edits.
Something feels off in the event graph—do a sanity pass: exports or dumps are fine, but don't leave obvious broken links or orphan execution if you can help it.
Try to compile whatever we touched. If the editor says you can't do that from here, explain what happened in plain language and what you'd do next.
Give me a tight recap: which assets we touched, whether anything still looks unhappy in the compiler, and one thing you'd test in PIE if you were me.
